ab: I kalafian anini, o maminokay ko safa to fa’inayan.
ch: 今天傍晚，弟弟就要回家了。
en: My younger brother is going home this evening.

ab: Tayra i icifa mi’aca to koko’ ci fofo ato kako, misaday to sakalafi no safa to fa’inayan.
ch: 我和奶奶去市場買雞，準備煮好吃的晚餐給弟弟。
en: My grandma and I went to the market to buy chickens and prepared a delicious dinner for my younger brother.

ab: Mita’elif kami to pi’acaan to dateng, adihay ko faelohay a dateng noya pa’aca’ay.
ch: 我們經過了菜攤，菜攤有好多新鮮的青菜。
en: We passed the vegetable stand, which had a lot of fresh green vegetables.

ab: Mi’aca to kami to incay, koreto ato saytaw.
ch: 我們買了空心菜、山藥和胡蘿蔔。
en: We bought water spinach, yams and carrots.

ab: Oya pi’acaan to losay a tawki sowal sa, “koesanay ko onglay ato lopas ako, mi’aca to miming makenaay saw!”
ch: 旁邊水果攤的老闆說:「我的鳳梨和桃子很甜。買一些吧!」
en: The owner of the nearby fruit stand said: “My pineapples and peaches are very sweet. Buy some!”

ab: Mi’aca to kami itiya to onglay ato lopas.
Mita’elif to kami toya pi’acaan to koko’, sowal sa koya tawki, “O maan ko ’a’acaen iso? ta’angay a masoeso ko koko’ anini, tolo so’ot ko cecay a kakoko’an.”
ch: 我們經過了肉攤，老闆說:「你要買什麼?今天的雞又大又肥，一隻300元。」
en: We passed the meat stall, and the boss said, “What do you want to buy? Today’s chickens are big and fat, and one is 300 NTD.”

ab: ’Atekaken cifofo, sakaemeden ko ’aca mamanay han nira ko tawki a milicay.
ch: 奶奶覺得太貴了，問老闆可不可以便宜一點。
en: Grandma thought it was too expensive and asked the boss if it could be cheaper.

ab: O sakaemeday to ko ’aca saan ko sowal no tawki, cowa to ko nganga’ay a lowanen.
ch: 但老闆說已經很便宜，不能再減了。
en: But the boss said that it is already very cheap and cannot be reduced any further.

ab: Oya tatihiay a tawki sowal sa, katayni mi’aca to futing ako, kaemeday a ta’angay ko futing no mako.
ch: 隔壁的老闆說，來買我的魚，我的魚又大又便宜。
en: The boss next door said, come and buy my fish, my fish is big and cheap.

ab: Ikol to, o koko’ o futing ko ma’acaay niyam, tahaloma’ tangtangen tomes sa ko dateng i eto.
ch: 最後我們買了雞也買了魚，回家煮了一桌子滿滿的菜。
en: In the end we bought chicken and fish, and went home to cook a table of food.

ab: Halafin to ko pitatala, to’eman sato ko romi’ad.
en: After waiting for a long time, it was already dark.

ab: Toya pitatalaan, ira ko tingwa no safa to fa’inayan sowal sa, malipalaw ira ko latoh no kalasofitayan, cowa kanga’ay pasera’ ko sofitay sa. Liyaw hanto no pa’aliwacan a minokay.
ch: 在等待中，弟弟打電話回來說，軍隊突然不能放假了，要下一個禮拜日才回來。
en: The younger brother called back and said that the army suddenly couldn’t take a vacation and would not be back until the next Sunday.

ab: Sowal sa ci fofo adihay ko mitangtangan pohayen ko salawinawina malacafay a komaen!
ch: 奶奶說已經煮了這麼多，叫親戚一起來吃吧!
en: Grandma said that she has cooked so much, so let’s eat together!

ab: Tahidangen to niyam ko salawinawina ato widawidang, malacafay a komaen tona sakadofah sanay a sakalafi.
ch: 我們邀請了親朋好友，一起來享用豐盛的晚餐。
en: We invited friends and family to have a great dinner together.
